local_surfaces.notes: Shared GEN3 Launchpad baseline. Builder-first surface for Workspace Builders (decision 0047). Admin Organizace flows, production workspace user entrypoints and deploy/server configuration live in Conglomerate Dashboard GEN3. Company apps declare ports in their package.json manifests.
User-facing shared Guide over the whole Conglomerate: explains Launchpad root, Organizations, workspaces, productionspace, personalspace and links into Organization-specific manuals.
Technical manual for Launchpad root maintenance agents.
Local helper drafts. Not long-term company knowledge.

forbidden_transfers: business strategy of one company as truth of another
module data without explicit reason and access

terminology.conglomerate: Multiple Organizations GEN3 under one Launchpad root on one machine, defined by launchpad.gen3.json. Not a business repo: each Organization keeps its own access boundary. Organizations are discovered by scanning organizations/*/company.gen3.json, never from a hardcoded registry in this shared file.
terminology.organization_gen3: One company = one super-repo = one GitHub organization = one access boundary, defined by company.gen3.json and mounted as organizations/<Organization>_GEN3/. The internal Organization slug stays clean and generation-free. Inside one Organization live logical Teams, the flat workspace/ module layer, org-level productionspace/ for production systems and modules.
terminology.workspace: The physical flat module mount layer inside one Organization GEN3, not a Team or an access boundary of its own. Teams are logical N:M groupings declared in company.gen3.json: one Team can reference multiple modules and one module can be referenced by multiple Teams. Hosted patterns are defined by each module or Organization rather than inferred from a workspace slug.
terminology.module: Nested Git repo with one domain source of truth, described in modules.manifest.json and company.gen3.json.modules. A module is owned by exactly one workspace. A missing module is not automatically an error: distinguish available, missing_access and planned_slot.
terminology.launchpad_root: Root folder of one Conglomerate on one machine, defined by launchpad.gen3.json. Mounts organizations/<org>/ and personalspace/ and hosts the shared Launchpad GEN3 and Guide. Exactly one Launchpad root per machine. This file is shared across every GEN3 user, so it carries only the generic contract — no machine-specific organization list, template list or personalspace owner.
terminology.personalspace: Private personal repository/mount owned by the machine owner or autonomous AI colleague GitHub account, outside shared GitHub organizations. It should grow into a private module layer analogous to workspace modules: per-user/per-colleague apps, Buddy runtime, personal tools and hosted/readable GBrain surfaces, with strict privacy and scoped agent access.
terminology.productionspace: Inside one Organization GEN3: the org-level place where the Organization's deployed/production systems live (e.g. firmware, connect, a product monorepo, public docs/website) — modules that are already deployed on a server / have their own CI-CD or are public, no longer worked on only locally or via a Workspace Host. A module is PROMOTED here from a workspace (versioned, e.g. v1.4) once a workspace version is deemed good. Unlike the workspace, productionspace has NO universal pattern: each production module keeps its OWN historically-designed release/CI-CD pipeline and rules. Mounted next to workspace mountpoints, shared by the whole Organization, owned by no single workspace. NOT the Workspace Host (a remote-access runtime for the workspace dílna). The Conglomerate-level Launchpad is SHARED (updated to everyone) and holds only universal principles, so it does NOT standardize or operate productionspace internals — each Organization defines its productionspace ITS OWN way in its own repo. Launchpad SHOWS a read-only productionspace overview for Workspace Builders (declared systems, git state; decision 0047), but does not operate it: no install/start/release actions without that Organization's explicit policy. How a given productionspace works is the Organization's concern, not Launchpad's.
terminology.conglomerate_dashboard: Hosted HumanAndMachine surface for Admin Organizace (Organization Admin) flows, Organization and AI-colleague configuration, access, billing, user entry into production workspace applications, and deploy/server configuration for workspace and personalspace applications. It applies changes through Git/adapter flows and should not become a hidden replacement source of truth.
